name: Government Of India Post-Matric Scholarship
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

additional_conditions: Only 2 professional courses allowed
If student fails first time, eligible for exam fees and maintenance
If student fails second time in same class, not eligible
Applicable outside Maharashtra as per GOI rules

special_conditions: additional_allowances: Disability-based allowances as per GR

name: Post-Matric Tuition Fee and Examination Fee (Freeship)
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

additional_conditions: Institute must be located in Maharashtra and government recognized
For professional courses, admission must be through CAP round
Only 1 failure is allowed in entire curriculum

name: Maintenance Allowance for student Studying in professional courses
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

additional_conditions: Student must be studying in a professional course
Student must be a beneficiary of Government of India Post-Matric Scholarship
Student must be residing in hostel (Government / Institute / outside)

documents_required: Registration/Application ID with GOI Post Matric Scholarship Scheme

name: Rajarshri Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Merit Scholarship
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for persons with disability
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

additional_conditions: Student must be studying in a recognized institution
Scheme applicable only once per education level
Student must not be employed full-time
Student must complete the course (not discontinue)
Applicable outside Maharashtra if domicile is Maharashtra
Distance education students eligible for limited benefits
Medical PG students with internship/stipend are eligible

special_conditions: additional_benefits: ['Study tour allowance up to 500 per year', 'Project printing/typing allowance up to 600 per year', 'Distance education students get 500 per year for books']

failure_rules.group_A: 1 failure allowed, second failure not allowed

documents_required: Gap Certificate (mandatory for Group A if gap exists)

name: Vocational Training Fee reimbursement for the students belonging to Scheduled Caste category Students
department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department

income_slabs.benefit: As per Government Of India Post-Matric Scholarship

additional_conditions: Admission must be through PPP scheme
Admission must be through centralized admission process
No scholarship for management quota admission
Course must be approved by DGT or MSCVT
Candidate must not have taken benefit for same course previously
Candidate must not have taken benefit from any other training program
Attendance is mandatory
Candidate must attend all exams
If academic progress is unsatisfactory, candidate becomes ineligible
Orphan candidates require recommendation letter

special_conditions: details: Up to 100% course fee reimbursement based on income and SSC status

renewal_policy: Student must be promoted to next year
Attendance must be as per DGT criteria

name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme (Government Of India)

additional_conditions: Applicable only for Scheduled Tribe students
If student has two consecutive drops, not eligible

renewal_policy: Student must pass previous year examination
If student fails, scholarship not granted for that year
Student must apply for renewal

additional_conditions: Applicable only for Scheduled Tribe students
State sponsored scheme

benefits.note: As per approved college fee structure

renewal_policy: Student must pass previous year examination
If student fails, scholarship not granted for that year

name: Vocational Education Fee Reimbursement

additional_conditions: Applicable only for Scheduled Tribe students
Applicable for vocational and professional courses

benefits.note: As per approved college fee structure

renewal_policy: Student must pass previous year examination

additional_conditions: Applicable only for Scheduled Tribe students

name: Post Matric Scholarship to VJNT Students
department: OBC, SEBC, VJNT & SBC Welfare Department

family_constraints: Maximum 2 male children eligible; no limit for female children
additional_conditions: CAP admission required only for professional courses
Course must be government approved
75% attendance is mandatory
Applicant must not receive another scholarship simultaneously
Course change allowed from non-professional to professional only
Scholarship continues only in same academic progression path

special_conditions: If admitted in government hostel, only 1/3rd allowance applicable
Full benefit applicable for B.Ed and D.Ed courses
Allowance applicable from admission date (before 20th rule applies)

failure_rules.group_A: 1 failure allowed, subsequent failures not allowed
failure_rules.general: If fail, benefits only for that academic year

renewal_policy: Applicant must pass previous year examination
Medical exceptions may be considered with valid proof

Applicant must follow same academic progression path

Scholarship continues only in same academic progression path
